english: a present); also for ɣtóṅ-ba (gtong ba) to send, to send out, so at least in W.; further: W. *ja sal, šu-gu sal, deb-sal* (ja gsal, shu gu gsal, deb gsal) please to give me some tea, to lend me some paper, pray, give me change; or more pressingly: *ja sal gos* (ja gsal dgos) I earnestly request you for some tea etc., I entreat you to ...; *sal mi gos* (gsal mi dgos) I thank you, I do not want it; bká-stsal-ba (bka' stsal ba) v. sub bka (bka'); dṅós-grúb stsól-ba (dngos grub stsol ba) to bestow spiritual gifts (?). — 3. sometimes incorr. for bsál-ba (bsal ba) (sél-ba (sel ba)) to clean, to clear, to remove Dzl.
v. rtsód-pa (rtsod pa), rtsóm-pa (rtsom pa), sometimes incorr. for btsád-pa (btsad pa), btsám-pa (btsam pa).

vb. to love, sbst. love, affection, kindness, nearly the same as byáms-pa (byams pa), frq. preceded by sñiṅ (snying), resp. túgs (thugs), q. v.; brtsé-bas (brtse bas) out of love, kindness, e. g. ɣnáṅ-ba (gnang ba) to give something out of love; with love, lovingly, kindly, e. g. skyóṅ-ba (skyong ba) to protect; brtsé-bai tsig (brtse ba'i tshig) words of love, kind exhortations Glr.; brtsé-bai ṕyag-brís (brtse ba'i phyag bris) your very kind letter; sñiṅ-brtse-ba (snying brtse ba), resp. túgs-brtse-ba (thugs brtse ba) = brtsé-ba (brtse ba); brtsé-ba-čan (brtse ba can), brtse-ldán (brtse ldan) loving, affectionate, kind; brtse(-ba)-méd(-pa) (brtse (ba) med (pa)) unkind, unmerciful, ungracious; brse-ɣdúṅ (brtse gdung) love, affection, ṕa-má brtse-ɣdúṅ čé yaṅ ċi žíg bya (pha ma brtse gdung che yang ci zhig bya) what could even parental love do? Glr.; lha-ṕrùg ɣžón-nui brtse-ɣdúṅ de (lha phrug gzhon nu'i brtse gdung de) this proof of love on the part of young goddesses towards me Mil.

1. vb. with la (la), to strive, to aim at, to exert one's self for, tsógs-pa-la (tshogs pa la) an accumulation of merits, frq.; brtsón-par byéd-pa (brtson par byed pa), or gyúr-ba (gyur ba), also with mṅón-par (mngon par) preceding it; to apply one's self, lás-la (las la) to business, túgs-dám-la (thugs dam la) to meditation Dzl., Mil. — 2. sbst. (Ssk. वीर्य, virtus) endeavour, effort, care, exertion, byá-ba-la brtsón-pa (bya ba la brtson pa) alacrity, readiness to act Wdṅ.; more frq. brtson-°grús (brtson grus) v. below. — 3. adj. = brtsón-pa-čan (brtson pa can), brtson-ldán (brtson ldan) Mil., diligent, assiduous, studious, sgrúb(-pa)-la (sgrub (pa) la) eager to obtain power over demons Mil.; brtsón-par (brtson par) on purpose, with intention, wilfully; as sbst. mostly brtson-°grús (brtson grus), with skyéd-pa (skyed pa), byéd-pa (byed pa), rtsóm-pa (rtsom pa) to use diligence, to show energy, zeal etc.; brtson-°grús drág-po (brtson grus drag po) intense application; brtson-°grús-čan (brtson grus can) assiduous, studious, brtson-°grus ñáms-te (brtson grus nyams te) Stg. having lost one's energy.
1. the letter tsa (tsha), the aspirate of ཙ (tsa) (cf. ཅ (ca)), sounded ts. — 2. num. fig.: 18.
1. hot, v. tsá-ba (tsha ba). — 2. grandchild, v. tsá-bo (tsha bo). — 3. v. tsa-tsa (tsha tsha). — 4. resp. illness, complaint C.

salt, tswa débs-pa (tshwa 'debs pa) to salt, with la (la); *tsa nyén-če* (tshwa nyen che) W. to taste, to try, food prepared with salt; k̄a-ru-tswa (kha ru tshwa) alum Med.; rgya-tswa (rgya tshwa) sal-ammoniac Med.; lče-myaṅ-tswa (lce myang tshwa) alum Lt.; rdo-tswa (rdo tshwa) rock-salt Cs.; ba-tswa (ba tshwa) impure soda, v. bá-mo (ba mo). — bód-tswa (bod tshwa) Lt.? — lán-tswa (lan tshwa) = tswa (tshwa). — tswa-k̄a (tshwa kha) salt mine Cs. — *tsa-(k̄u-)čan* (tshwa (khu) can) W. saline, salinous, — tswa-sgo (tshwa sgo) place where salt is found. — *tsa-tsé* (tshwa tshe) sal-ammoniac C. — *tsa-ču* (tshwa chu) salt-water, brine; acc. to some, vinegar (?).
also čá-ga-bu (ca ga bu), tsag-tság (tshag tshag) grasshopper, locust C.
